methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Ouster is a public semiconductor company founded by Angus Pacala that has raised over $500M and currently trades at a $1.5B valuation. The relevant peer universe consists of 3 public-stage Chips & Semiconductors companies: Cerebras ($23.0B), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is applicable and represents the primary valuation anchor. Across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables, the median valuation is $5.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $23.0B. This wide dispersion reflects material differences in market positioning and investor sentiment within the public semiconductor cohort. The public-comp anchoring method using Price-to-Sales multiples cannot be applied because Ouster's revenue is not disclosed; the broader semiconductor sector median P/S of 25.3x across 3 tickers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) would require current revenue data to yield a reliable implied valuation.

The triangulated valuation range supported by this analysis is $4.2B to $5.8B, centered on the $5.0B peer median. The current $1.5B market valuation sits substantially below this range, suggesting a potential valuation gap. Key uncertainties include the limited peer set (3 comps with extreme dispersion), the absence of revenue-based triangulation, and the lack of visibility into Ouster's growth trajectory and profitability relative to peers. A more robust analysis would benefit from revenue disclosure or higher-frequency comparable transaction data.
